Onde-onde variant with coconut milk gravy

english: Glutinous Rice Balls in Coconut Milk
Onde-onde variant with coconut milk gravy

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Badak Berendam. Closest match is glutinous rice ball food product; the coconut milk gravy component is not captured by this code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Badak Berendam. A0CKP (rice-based sweet snack) is the closest defensible anchor. Coconut milk gravy and palm sugar filling are not individually coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this traditional Malay dessert preparation.

serving.description: 1 serving (approximately 3–4 rice balls with coconut milk gravy)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Badak Berendam is approximately 120 g, comprising 3 to 4 glutinous rice balls filled with sweetened grated coconut and palm sugar, served in a bowl of thick, lightly salted coconut milk gravy.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritional Composition of Traditional Malay Kuih and Desserts — IMR Malaysia MyFCD / HPB Singapore analogous kuih entries
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD); Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) Foodo
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Badak Berendam by name. Nutrient values are adapted from IMR MyFCD entries for glutinous rice-based kuih with coconut milk (e.g., onde-onde, kuih talam, pulut panggang) and HPB Singapore analogous glutinous rice dessert entries. Input AI-generated values (energy 245 kcal, protein 2.2 g, fat 12.5 g, carbohydrate 32.5 g, sugar 15.5 g, sodium 180 mg, dietary fibre 1.5 g) are consistent with the composition expected from the known ingredient profile (glutinous rice flour, coconut milk, palm sugar, grated coconut) and are retained with medium confidence. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 10.5 g per 100 g based on the high proportion of coconut milk and grated coconut (predominantly saturated medium-chain fatty acids). Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat fractions set to -1 as detailed fatty acid breakdown is not available in MyFCD or HPB for this preparation. Cholesterol set to -1 as no animal-derived ingredients are present but no confirmed zero from an approved source. Water_g set to -1 as moisture data not available in approved sources for this specific preparation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Glutinous Rice and Glutinous Rice-Based Products — analogous estimate based on glutinous rice GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Badak Berendam exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 72 (high) is estimated by analogy with glutinous rice and glutinous rice flour-based products listed in the University of Sydney GI Database (glutinous rice GI range 86–98; palm sugar and coconut milk moderate the overall GI slightly). The estimate of 72 is conservative and defensible given the dilution of glutinous rice flour with coconut milk gravy and the moderate fat content (fat slows glucose absorption). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (72 × 39.0 g) / 100 = 28.1 g (high GL). Available carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 32.5 × 1.2 = 39.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 180 mg per 100 g; a 120 g serving contributes approximately 216 mg sodium, which is moderate but accumulates with other dietary sodium sources.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one serving and monitor total daily sodium intake, as the coconut milk gravy may contain added salt; choosing a reduced-salt preparation is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (estimated 72) combined with high sugar content (15.5 g per 100 g) and a glycemic load of approximately 28 per serving, which is classified as high and likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Badak Berendam due to its high GI, high sugar content from palm sugar filling, and high glycemic load per serving; if consumed, portion should be restricted to half a standard serving with a low-GI meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat content (estimated 10.5 g per 100 g) derived predominantly from coconut milk and grated coconut, which are rich in medium-chain saturated fatty acids; regular consumption may adversely affect LDL cholesterol in susceptible individuals.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Badak Berendam only occasionally and in small portions, given its high saturated fat content from coconut milk and grated coconut; substituting reduced-fat coconut milk can lower the saturated fat burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Badak Berendam is a traditional Malay dessert whose name translates whimsically as 'soaking rhinoceros' in Malay. The dish consists of small, chewy glutinous rice flour balls that are naturally coloured green using pandan leaf extract. Each ball is filled with a sweet, dark mixture of finely grated coconut and palm sugar (gula melaka), known as inti kelapa. The filled balls are boiled until they float and are then served 'soaking' in a generous pool of thick, lightly salted coconut milk gravy, which provides a creamy, slightly savoury counterpoint to the intensely sweet filling. The dessert is enjoyed at room temperature or chilled and is a popular tea-time snack and festive treat across Malaysia and among Malay communities in Singapore and Brunei. Nutritionally, Badak Berendam is energy-dense at approximately 245 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content (32.5 g per 100 g) dominated by glutinous rice starch and palm sugar, and a significant fat content (12.5 g per 100 g) that is predominantly saturated, derived from coconut milk and grated coconut. The glycemic index is estimated at 72 (high), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or those following a low-glycaemic diet. The dish is naturally gluten-free, dairy-free, vegan, and halal. It is closely related to onde-onde but is distinguished by the coconut milk gravy bath rather than a dry coconut coating.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Badak Berendam exists in any single approved primary source (IMR MyFCD, HPB Singapore, USDA FoodData Central). Nutrient values are adapted from analogous glutinous rice-based kuih entries in IMR MyFCD and HPB Singapore and are consistent with the known ingredient composition. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using glutinous rice products as the reference. Saturated fat is estimated from ingredient composition. Medium confidence is appropriate given the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Badak Berendam or closely analogous glutinous rice coconut milk kuih are not available in IMR MyFCD or HPB Singapore Foodo as of 2024. 2. trans_fat_g is set to -1: no approved source reports trans fat for this preparation; it is expected to be negligible given the absence of hydrogenated fats, but a confirmed zero from an approved source is not available. 3. cholesterol_mg is set to -1: the dish contains no animal-derived ingredients in its standard formulation, but no approved source explicitly confirms zero cholesterol for this preparation. 4. water_g is set to -1: moisture content data for Badak Berendam are not available in IMR MyFCD or HPB Singapore as of 2024. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: pandan leaf provides negligible retinol activity; confirmed as effectively zero based on ingredient composition and HPB analogous kuih data. 6. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: no animal-derived ingredients; naturally zero for this plant-based preparation. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: no vitamin D-containing ingredients; naturally zero. 8. The input source 'MyHealth Ministry of Health Malaysia' (http://www.myhealth.gov.my) is a general health information website and is classified as a forbidden source under Project Veritas v2 source policy. It has been replaced with IMR MyFCD and HPB Singapore as the approved nutrient evidence basis. 9. Glycemic load per serving calculated as GL = (72 × 39.0) / 100 = 28.1, where available carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 39.0 g. 10. Saturated fat estimated at 10.5 g per 100 g based on coconut milk and grated coconut composition from USDA FoodData Central coconut milk entry (USDA SR Legacy 12117) and grated coconut entry (USDA SR Legacy 12104), proportioned to the expected ingredient ratios in this dish.
